ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُصْعَبٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مَالِكُ بْنُ أَنَسٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الزُّهْرِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيَّبِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏رَجُلًا ‏ ‏أَتَى النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ إِنَّ امْرَأَتِي وَلَدَتْ غُلَامًا أَسْوَدَ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏هَلْ لَكَ مِنْ إِبِلٍ قَالَ نَعَمْ قَالَ فَمَا أَلْوَانُهَا قَالَ ‏ ‏رُمْكٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَرُبَّمَا لَيْسَ جَاءَتْ بِالْبَعِيرِ ‏ ‏الْأَوْرَقِ ‏ ‏قَالَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ نَعَمْ قَالَ فَأَنَّى ‏ ‏تَرَى ذَلِكَ قَالَ أُرَاهُ ‏ ‏نَزَعَهُ ‏ ‏عِرْقٌ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَهَذَا ‏ ‏نَزَعَهُ ‏ ‏عِرْقٌ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Mus'ab narrated to us, Malik ibn Anas narrated to us, from al-Zuhri, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), that a man came to the Prophet ﷺ and said: 'I am ruined, O Messenger of Allah.' He said: 'What has ruined you?' He said: 'I had relations with my wife during Ramadan [while fasting].' He said: 'Can you free a slave?' He said: 'No.' He said: 'Can you fast two consecutive months?' He said: 'No.' He said: 'Can you feed sixty poor people?' He said: 'No.' Then a large basket of dates was brought to the Prophet ﷺ. He said: 'Take this and give it in charity.' The man said: 'To someone poorer than us? There is no household between the two lava fields [i.e., in all of Madinah] more in need of it than us.' The Prophet ﷺ laughed until his premolars appeared and said: 'Go and feed your family.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُصْعَبٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْأَوْزَاعِيُّ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الزُّهْرِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏ثَابِتٍ الزُّرَقِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كُنَّا مَعَ ‏ ‏عُمَرَ بْنِ الْخَطَّابِ ‏ ‏بِطَرِيقِ ‏ ‏مَكَّةَ ‏ ‏إِذْ هَاجَتْ رِيحٌ فَقَالَ لِمَنْ حَوْلَهُ الرِّيحَ قَالَ فَلَمْ يَرُدُّوا عَلَيْهِ شَيْئًا قَالَ فَبَلَغَنِي الَّذِي سَأَلَ عَنْهُ مِنْ ذَلِكَ فَاسْتَحْثَثْتُ رَاحِلَتِي حَتَّى أَدْرَكْتُهُ فَقُلْتُ يَا أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ بَلَغَنِي أَنَّكَ سَأَلْتَ عَنْ الرِّيحِ وَإِنِّي سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏الرِّيحُ مِنْ رَوْحِ اللَّهِ فَلَا تَسُبُّوهَا وَسَلُوا خَيْرَهَا وَاسْتَعِيذُوا بِهِ مِنْ شَرِّهَا ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Mus'ab narrated to us, he said al-Awza'i narrated to us, from al-Zuhri, from Thabit al-Zuraqi, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه) who said: We were with Umar ibn al-Khattab (رضي الله عنه) when the Prophet ﷺ said: 'In a night I was taken on a journey (Isra'), and I saw Musa (عليه السلام) — a dark-skinned, tall man, as if he were from the men of [the tribe of] Shanu'ah. And I saw Isa ibn Maryam (عليه السلام) — of medium build, reddish-white. And the closest person I have seen resembling him is Urwah ibn Mas'ud.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَابِرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏يَزِيدَ بْنَ الْأَصَمِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كُنْتُ ‏ ‏بِالْمَدِينَةِ ‏ ‏مَعَ ‏ ‏مَرْوَانَ بْنِ الْحَكَمِ ‏ ‏وَأَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏فَمَرَّتْ بِهِمَا جِنَازَةٌ فَقَامَ ‏ ‏أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏وَلَمْ يَقُمْ ‏ ‏مَرْوَانُ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ ‏ ‏أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏إِنِّي رَأَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَرَّتْ بِهِ جِنَازَةٌ ‏ ‏فَقَامَ فَقَامَ عِنْدَ ذَلِكَ ‏ ‏مَرْوَانُ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, he said Shu'bah narrated to us, from Jabir, he said I heard Yazid ibn al-Asamm, he said: I was in Madinah with Marwan ibn al-Hakam when we heard a man at the minbar of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ narrating from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ who said: 'By the One in Whose Hand is my soul, the Hour will not come until the sun rises from its place of setting. On the day it rises, all people will believe, but that is when "no soul will benefit from its faith if it had not believed before" [6:158].'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مَعْمَرٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الزُّهْرِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيَّبِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ نَهَى عَنْ ‏ ‏الْفَرَعِ ‏ ‏وَالْعَتِيرَةِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدٌ ‏ ‏وَقَدْ سَمِعْتُهُ أَنَا مِنْ ‏ ‏مَعْمَرٍ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, he said Shu'bah narrated to us, from Ma'mar, from al-Zuhri, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), from the Prophet ﷺ who said: 'Ibrahim (عليه السلام) circumcised himself with an adze (qaddum) when he was eighty years old.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏أَبَا هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏الْوَلَدُ لِصَاحِبِ ‏ ‏الْفِرَاشِ ‏ ‏وَلِلْعَاهِرِ ‏ ‏الْحَجَرُ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, Shu'bah narrated to us, from Muhammad ibn Ziyad, he said I heard Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه) saying: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: 'Name yourselves with my name, but do not use my kunyah.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏أَبَا هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏يُحَدِّثُ ‏ ‏أَنَّ نَبِيَّ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ لِكُلِّ نَبِيٍّ دَعْوَةٌ دَعَا بِهَا فِي أُمَّتِهِ فَيُسْتَجَابُ لَهُ وَإِنِّي ‏ ‏أُرِيدُ إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ أَنْ أُؤَخِّرَ دَعْوَتِي ‏ ‏شَفَاعَةً ‏ ‏لِأُمَّتِي يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, he said Shu'bah narrated to us, from Muhammad ibn Ziyad, he said I heard Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه) narrating, that the Prophet of Allah ﷺ said: 'I swear by Allah, if one of you were to take his rope in the morning and go to the mountain and gather firewood, then sell it and eat from it and give charity, it would be better for him than asking people.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏كَانَ ‏ ‏أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏يَأْتِي عَلَى النَّاسِ وَهُمْ يَتَوَضَّئُونَ فِي الْمَطْهَرَةِ فَيَقُولُ لَهُمْ ‏ ‏أَسْبِغُوا ‏ ‏الْوُضُوءَ ‏ ‏أَسْبِغُوا ‏ ‏الْوُضُوءَ فَإِنِّي سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏أَبَا الْقَاسِمِ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏وَيْلٌ ‏ ‏لِلْأَعْقَابِ ‏ ‏مِنْ النَّارِ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, he said Shu'bah narrated to us, from Muhammad ibn Ziyad, who said: Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه) used to come to the people while they were performing wudu from a vessel and say: 'Perfect the wudu, perfect the wudu! Abu al-Qasim ﷺ said: "Woe to the heels from the Hellfire."'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كَانَ ‏ ‏مَرْوَانُ ‏ ‏يَسْتَعْمِلُ ‏ ‏أَبَا هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏عَلَى ‏ ‏الْمَدِينَةِ ‏ ‏قَالَ فَكَانَ إِذَا رَأَى إِنْسَانًا يَجُرُّ إِزَارَهُ ضَرَبَ بِرِجْلِهِ ثُمَّ يَقُولُ قَدْ جَاءَ الْأَمِيرُ قَدْ جَاءَ الْأَمِيرُ ثُمَّ يَقُولُ قَالَ ‏ ‏أَبُو الْقَاسِمِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لَا يَنْظُرُ اللَّهُ إِلَى مَنْ جَرَّ ‏ ‏إِزَارَهُ ‏ ‏بَطَرًا ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, Shu'bah narrated to us, from Muhammad ibn Ziyad, he said: Marwan used to appoint Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه) over Madinah. He said: And when he led us in Friday prayer, he would supplicate for the Prophet ﷺ, Abu Bakr, and Umar (رضي الله عنهما), and he would say: 'O Allah, forgive the believing men and believing women, the Muslim men and Muslim women.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏أَبَا هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏يُحَدِّثُ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَحْفِهِمَا جَمِيعًا أَوْ أَنْعِلْهُمَا جَمِيعًا فَإِذَا لَبِسْتَ فَابْدَأْ بِالْيَمِينِ وَإِذَا خَلَعْتَ فَابْدَأْ بِالْيُسْرَى ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, he said Shu'bah narrated to us, from Muhammad ibn Ziyad, he said I heard Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه) narrating, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'By the One in Whose Hand is my soul, I was about to order firewood to be collected, then order for the prayer to be called, then order a man to lead the people in prayer, then go to those men [who stay away from the prayer] and burn their houses down upon them. By the One in Whose Hand is my soul, if any one of them knew that he would find a fat bone or two good hooves, he would attend the Isha prayer.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏أَبَا هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِذَا جَاءَ أَحَدَكُمْ خَادِمُهُ بِطَعَامٍ فَإِنْ لَمْ يُجْلِسْهُ مَعَهُ فَلْيُنَاوِلْهُ أُكْلَةً أَوْ أُكْلَتَيْنِ ‏ ‏أَوْ لُقْمَةً أَوْ لُقْمَتَيْنِ ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏شَكَّ ‏ ‏فَإِنَّهُ ‏ ‏وَلِيَ ‏ ‏عِلَاجَهُ ‏ ‏وَحَرَّهُ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, he said: Shu'bah narrated to us, from Muhammad ibn Ziyad, who said: I heard Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه) saying: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'When one of you is brought food by his servant, if he does not seat him with him, then let him hand him a morsel or two — Shu'bah was uncertain — for he is the one who endured its preparation and its heat.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏الْحَسَنَ ‏ ‏أَخَذَ تَمْرَةً مِنْ تَمْرِ ‏ ‏الصَّدَقَةِ ‏ ‏فَجَعَلَهَا فِي ‏ ‏فِيهِ فَقَالَ لَهُ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏كِخْ كِخْ أَلْقِهَا أَمَا شَعَرْتَ ‏ ‏أَنَّا أَهْلَ بَيْتٍ لَا نَأْكُلُ ‏ ‏الصَّدَقَةَ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, he said: Shu'bah narrated to us, from Muhammad ibn Ziyad, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), that al-Hasan took a date from the dates of charity and put it in his mouth. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to him: 'Kikh, kikh! Spit it out! Do you not know that we, the people of the household, do not eat from charity?'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَوْ ‏ ‏أَبُو الْقَاسِمِ ‏ ‏لَوْ أَنَّ ‏ ‏الْأَنْصَارَ ‏ ‏سَلَكُوا وَادِيًا ‏ ‏أَوْ ‏ ‏شِعْبًا ‏ ‏وَسَلَكَ النَّاسُ وَادِيًا ‏ ‏أَوْ ‏ ‏شِعْبًا ‏ ‏لَسَلَكْتُ وَادِيَ ‏ ‏الْأَنْصَارِ ‏ ‏وَلَوْلَا الْهِجْرَةُ لَكُنْتُ امْرَأً مِنْ ‏ ‏الْأَنْصَارِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏فَكَانَ ‏ ‏أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏مَا ظَلَمَ بِأَبِي وَأُمِّي لَقَدْ آوَوْهُ وَنَصَرُوهُ وَكَلِمَةً أُخْرَى ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us: Shu'bah narrated to us, from Muhammad ibn Ziyad, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ — or Abu al-Qasim — said: 'If the Ansar were to take a valley or a mountain pass, and the rest of the people took another valley or mountain pass, I would take the valley of the Ansar. Were it not for the Hijrah, I would have been a man of the Ansar.' Abu Hurayrah used to say: 'He did them no injustice — by my father and mother — for they gave him shelter and supported him,' and another word.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْمُغِيرَةِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏إِبْرَاهِيمَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏لَا ‏ ‏تُصَرُّوا ‏ ‏الْإِبِلَ وَالْغَنَمَ فَمَنْ اشْتَرَى ‏ ‏مُصَرَّاةً ‏ ‏فَهُوَ بِأَحَدِ ‏ ‏النَّظَرَيْنِ ‏ ‏إِنْ شَاءَ رَدَّهَا وَرَدَّ مَعَهَا ‏ ‏صَاعًا ‏ ‏مِنْ تَمْرٍ قَالَ وَلَا يَبِيعُ الرَّجُلُ عَلَى بَيْعِ أَخِيهِ وَلَا تَسْأَلُ الْمَرْأَةُ طَلَاقَ أُخْتِهَا ‏ ‏لِتَكْتَفِئَ ‏ ‏مَا فِي صَحْفَتِهَا فَإِنَّ مَالَهَا مَا كُتِبَ لَهَا وَلَا ‏ ‏تَنَاجَشُوا ‏ ‏وَلَا ‏ ‏تَلَقَّوْا الْأَجْلَابَ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, he said: Shu'bah narrated to us, from al-Mughirah, from Ibrahim, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: 'Do not withhold milk from camels and sheep to deceive buyers. Whoever buys such a musarrah animal has the option of two choices: if he wishes, he may return it along with a sa' of dates.' He also said: 'A man should not sell over the sale of his brother, and a woman should not ask for the divorce of her sister in order to tip over what is in her plate, for her provision is what has been decreed for her. And do not engage in najash (bidding up prices to deceive), and do not intercept goods coming to market.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مَنْصُورٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي حَازِمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏مَنْ حَجَّ هَذَا ‏ ‏الْبَيْتَ ‏ ‏فَلَمْ ‏ ‏يَرْفُثْ ‏ ‏وَلَمْ يَفْسُقْ رَجَعَ كَمَا وَلَدَتْهُ أُمُّهُ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us: Shu'bah narrated to us, from Mansur, from Abu Hazim, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: 'Whoever performs Hajj to this House and does not engage in obscenity or wickedness, he returns as the day his mother bore him.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏سُهَيْلُ بْنُ أَبِي صَالِحٍ ‏ ‏يُحَدِّثُ عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏لَا وُضُوءَ إِلَّا مِنْ ‏ ‏حَدَثٍ ‏ ‏أَوْ رِيحٍ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us: Shu'bah narrated to us, who said: Suhayl ibn Abi Salih narrated from his father, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: 'There is no need for wudu except from hadath (breaking wind or impurity) or passing wind.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَيَّارٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي حَازِمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَنْ حَجَّ هَذَا ‏ ‏الْبَيْتَ ‏ ‏فَلَمْ ‏ ‏يَرْفُثْ ‏ ‏وَلَمْ يَفْسُقْ رَجَعَ كَمَا وَلَدَتْهُ أُمُّهُ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, he said: Shu'bah narrated to us, from Sayyar, from Abu Hazim, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever performs Hajj to this House and does not engage in obscenity or wickedness, he returns as the day his mother bore him.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ دِينَارٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏سُلَيْمَانَ بْنَ يَسَارٍ ‏ ‏يُحَدِّثُ عَنْ ‏ ‏عِرَاكِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏لَيْسَ عَلَى غُلَامِ الْمُسْلِمِ وَلَا عَلَى فَرَسِهِ ‏ ‏صَدَقَةٌ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, he said: Shu'bah narrated to us, from 'Abdullah ibn Dinar, who said: I heard Sulayman ibn Yasar narrating from 'Irak ibn Malik, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, who said: 'There is no zakah due on a Muslim's slave or his horse.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ الْمُهَاجِرِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي الشَّعْثَاءِ الْمُحَارِبِيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كُنَّا قُعُودًا مَعَ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏فِي الْمَسْجِدِ ‏ ‏فَأَذَّنَ الْمُؤَذِّنُ فَقَامَ رَجُلٌ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ فَخَرَجَ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏أَمَّا هَذَا فَقَدْ عَصَى ‏ ‏أَبَا الْقَاسِمِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us: Shu'bah narrated to us, from Ibrahim ibn al-Muhajir, from Abu al-Sha'tha al-Muharibi, who said: We were sitting with Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه) in the mosque when the mu'adhdhin called the adhan. A man in the mosque stood up and left. Abu Hurayrah said: 'As for this one, he has disobeyed Abu al-Qasim ﷺ.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي حَصِينٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏ذَكْوَانَ أَبَا صَالِحٍ ‏ ‏يُحَدِّثُ عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَنْ رَآنِي فِي الْمَنَامِ فَقَدْ رَآنِي إِنَّ الشَّيْطَانَ لَا يَتَصَوَّرُ بِي ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏أَوْ قَالَ لَا يَتَشَبَّهُ بِي ‏ ‏وَمَنْ كَذَبَ عَلَيَّ مُتَعَمِّدًا ‏ ‏فَلْيَتَبَوَّأْ ‏ ‏مَقْعَدَهُ مِنْ النَّارِ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us: Shu'bah narrated to us, from Abu Hasin, who said: I heard Dhakwan Abu Salih narrating from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever sees me in a dream has truly seen me, for the Shaytan cannot assume my form' — Shu'bah said: or he said, 'cannot resemble me' — 'and whoever deliberately lies about me, let him take his seat in the Fire.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سُلَيْمَانَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏ذَكْوَانَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏كُلُّ مَوْلُودٍ يُولَدُ عَلَى ‏ ‏الْفِطْرَةِ ‏ ‏فَأَبَوَاهُ يُهَوِّدَانِهِ وَيُنَصِّرَانِهِ وَيُشْرِكَانِهِ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us: Shu'bah narrated to us, from Sulayman, from Dhakwan, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: 'Every child is born upon the fitrah (natural disposition of Islam), then his parents make him a Jew, or a Christian, or a polytheist.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي بِشْرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ شَقِيقٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏خَيْرُكُمْ ‏ ‏قَرْنِي ‏ ‏ثُمَّ الَّذِينَ يَلُونَهُمْ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏لَا أَدْرِي ذَكَرَ مَرَّتَيْنِ أَوْ ثَلَاثًا ‏ ‏ثُمَّ خَلَفَ مِنْ بَعْدِهِمْ قَوْمٌ يُحِبُّونَ السِّمَانَةَ يَشْهَدُونَ وَلَا يُسْتَشْهَدُونَ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, he said: Shu'bah narrated to us, from Abu Bishr, from 'Abdullah ibn Shaqiq, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: 'The best of you are my generation, then those who follow them.' Abu Hurayrah said: 'I do not know whether he mentioned two or three times.' Then after them will come people who love obesity, who testify without being asked to testify.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏سَعِيدَ بْنَ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ الْمَقْبُرِيَّ ‏ ‏يُحَدِّثُ عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَا أَسْفَلَ مِنْ الْكَعْبَيْنِ فَفِي النَّارِ ‏ ‏يَعْنِي ‏ ‏الْإِزَارَ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us: Shu'bah narrated to us, who said: I heard Sa'id ibn Abi Sa'id al-Maqburi narrating from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whatever of the izar (lower garment) hangs below the ankles is in the Fire.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏قَتَادَةَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏النَّضْرِ بْنِ أَنَسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏بَشِيرِ بْنِ نَهِيكٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏إِذَا أَفْلَسَ رَجُلٌ بِمَالِ قَوْمٍ فَرَأَى رَجُلٌ مَتَاعَهُ بِعَيْنِهِ فَهُوَ أَحَقُّ بِهِ مِنْ غَيْرِهِ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us: Shu'bah narrated to us, from Qatadah, from al-Nadr ibn Anas, from Bashir ibn Nahik, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, who said: 'When a man goes bankrupt owing debts to people, and a man finds his exact goods with him, he has more right to them than anyone else.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مَعْمَرٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏الزُّهْرِيُّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيَّبِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏خَمْسٌ مِنْ الْفِطْرَةِ ‏ ‏الْخِتَانُ ‏ ‏وَالِاسْتِحْدَادُ ‏ ‏وَنَتْفُ الْإِبْطِ وَتَقْلِيمُ الْأَظْفَارِ وَقَصُّ الشَّارِبِ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us: Ma'mar narrated to us, who said: al-Zuhri informed us, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Five are from the fitrah (natural disposition): circumcision, shaving the pubic hair, plucking the armpit hair, trimming the nails, and trimming the mustache.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هِشَامُ بْنُ حَسَّانَ الْقُرْدُوسِيُّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سِيرِينَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏الْحَسَنَةُ بِعَشْرِ أَمْثَالِهَا وَالصَّوْمُ لِي وَأَنَا ‏ ‏أَجْزِي ‏ ‏بِهِ يَدَعُ طَعَامَهُ وَشَرَابَهُ مِنْ ‏ ‏جَرَّايَ ‏ ‏الصَّوْمُ لِي وَأَنَا ‏ ‏أَجْزِي ‏ ‏بِهِ ‏ ‏وَلَخُلُوفُ ‏ ‏فَمِ الصَّائِمِ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ أَطْيَبُ مِنْ رِيحِ الْمِسْكِ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us: Hisham ibn Hassan al-Qurdusi narrated to us, from Muhammad ibn Sirin, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: 'A good deed is rewarded tenfold, and fasting is for Me and I reward for it. He leaves his food and drink for My sake. Fasting is for Me and I reward for it. And the breath of the fasting person is more pleasant to Allah (عز وجل) than the fragrance of musk.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هِشَامُ بْنُ حَسَّانَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏يُوشِكُ مَنْ عَاشَ مِنْكُمْ أَنْ يَلْقَى ‏ ‏عِيسَى ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ ‏ ‏إِمَامًا مَهْدِيًّا وَحَكَمًا عَدْلًا فَيَكْسِرُ الصَّلِيبَ وَيَقْتُلُ الْخِنْزِيرَ وَيَضَعُ ‏ ‏الْجِزْيَةَ ‏ ‏وَتَضَعُ الْحَرْبُ ‏ ‏أَوْزَارَهَا ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, he said: Hisham ibn Hassan narrated to us, from Muhammad, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: 'It is near that those of you who live long will meet 'Isa ibn Maryam (عليه السلام) as a just leader and a fair judge. He will break the cross, kill the swine, abolish the jizyah, and war will lay down its burdens.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هِشَامٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏مَنْ رَآنِي فِي الْمَنَامِ فَقَدْ رَآنِي فَإِنَّ الشَّيْطَانَ لَا يَتَمَثَّلُ بِي ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us: Hisham narrated to us, from Muhammad, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: 'Whoever sees me in a dream has truly seen me, for the Shaytan cannot take my form.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هِشَامٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏مَنْ هَمَّ بِحَسَنَةٍ فَلَمْ يَعْمَلْهَا كُتِبَتْ لَهُ حَسَنَةٌ فَإِنْ عَمِلَهَا كُتِبَتْ لَهُ عَشْرُ أَمْثَالِهَا إِلَى سَبْعِ مِائَةٍ وَسَبْعِ أَمْثَالِهَا فَإِنْ لَمْ يَعْمَلْهَا كُتِبَتْ لَهُ حَسَنَةٌ وَمَنْ هَمَّ بِسَيِّئَةٍ فَلَمْ يَعْمَلْهَا لَمْ تُكْتَبْ عَلَيْهِ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, he said: Hisham narrated to us, from Muhammad, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: 'Whoever intends a good deed but does not do it, a good deed is recorded for him. If he does it, ten to seven hundred times its like are recorded for him. And whoever intends an evil deed but does not do it, it is not recorded against him.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هِشَامٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏الْفَأْرَةُ مِمَّا مُسِخَ ‏ ‏وَآيَةُ ‏ ‏ذَلِكَ أَنَّهُ يُوضَعُ لَهَا لَبَنُ ‏ ‏اللِّقَاحِ ‏ ‏فَلَا تَقْرَبُهُ وَإِذَا وُضِعَ لَهَا لَبَنُ الْغَنَمِ أَصَابَتْ مِنْهُ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ ‏ ‏لَهُ ‏ ‏كَعْبٌ ‏ ‏سَمِعْتَ هَذَا مِنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏فَأُنْزِلَتْ عَلَيَّ التَّوْرَاةُ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, he said: Hisham narrated to us, from Muhammad, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), who said: 'The mouse is from those that were transformed, and the sign of that is that when camel milk is placed before it, it does not approach it, but when sheep's milk is placed before it, it drinks from it.' Ka'b said to him: 'Did you hear this from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ?' He replied: 'Was the Torah revealed to me?'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هِشَامٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏الْبَهِيمَةُ ‏ ‏عَقْلُهَا ‏ ‏جُبَارٌ ‏ ‏وَالْبِئْرُ ‏ ‏جُبَارٌ ‏ ‏وَفِي ‏ ‏الرِّكَازِ ‏ ‏الْخُمُسُ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, he said: Hisham narrated to us, from Muhammad, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: 'An animal's kick is jubar (no liability), a well is jubar (no liability), and in rikaz (buried treasure) there is one-fifth (to be given as zakah).'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُوسَى بْنِ أَبِي عُثْمَانَ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏أَبَا عُثْمَانَ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏أَبَا هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏الْمُؤَذِّنُ يُغْفَرُ لَهُ ‏ ‏مَدَّ ‏ ‏صَوْتِهِ وَيَشْهَدُ لَهُ كُلُّ رَطْبٍ وَيَابِسٍ وَشَاهِدُ الصَّلَاةِ يُكْتَبُ لَهُ خَمْسٌ وَعِشْرُونَ حَسَنَةً وَيُكَفَّرُ عَنْهُ مَا بَيْنَهُمَا ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, he said: Shu'bah narrated to us, from Musa ibn Abi 'Uthman, who said: I heard Abu 'Uthman say: I heard Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه) saying: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The mu'adhdhin is forgiven to the extent of his voice, and every moist and dry thing bears witness for him. The one who attends the prayer has twenty-five good deeds written for him, and his sins between the two prayers are expiated.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِنَّمَا الْإِمَامُ ‏ ‏لِيُؤْتَمَّ ‏ ‏بِهِ فَإِذَا كَبَّرَ فَكَبِّرُوا وَإِذَا رَكَعَ فَارْكَعُوا وَإِذَا قَالَ سَمِعَ اللَّهُ لِمَنْ حَمِدَهُ فَقُولُوا رَبَّنَا لَكَ الْحَمْدُ وَإِنْ صَلَّى جَالِسًا فَصَلُّوا جُلُوسًا أَجْمَعُونَ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, he said: Muhammad ibn 'Amr narrated to us, from Abu Salamah, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The imam is appointed to be followed. So when he says the takbir, say the takbir. When he bows, bow. When he says "Sami'a Allahu liman hamidah," say "Rabbana lakal-hamd." And if he prays sitting, then pray sitting, all of you.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سَعِيدٌ يَعْنِي ابْنَ أَبِي عَرُوبَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي مُحَمَّدٍ أَظُنُّهُ حَبِيبُ بْنُ الشَّهِيدِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَطَاءِ بْنِ أَبِي رَبَاحٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏فِي كُلِّ الصَّلَوَاتِ يَقْرَأُ فِيهَا ‏ ‏فَمَا أَسْمَعَنَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَسْمَعْنَاكُمْ وَمَا أَخْفَى عَلَيْنَا أَخْفَيْنَا عَلَيْكُمْ ‏
en: Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, he said: Sa'id — meaning Ibn Abi 'Arubah — narrated to us, from Abu Muhammad, whom I think is Habib ibn al-Shahid, from 'Ata ibn Abi Rabah, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), who said: 'In all the prayers there is recitation. What the Messenger of Allah ﷺ made audible to us, we make audible to you, and what he kept silent from us, we keep silent from you.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْعَلَاءُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏الْعَيْنَانِ تَزْنِيَانِ وَاللِّسَانُ يَزْنِي وَالْيَدَانِ تَزْنِيَانِ وَالرِّجْلَانِ تَزْنِيَانِ وَيُحَقِّقُ ذَلِكَ أَوْ يُكَذِّبُهُ الْفَرْجُ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us, he said: 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Ibrahim narrated to me, he said: al-'Ala ibn 'Abd al-Rahman narrated to us, from his father, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The eyes commit zina (adultery), the tongue commits zina, the hands commit zina, the feet commit zina, and the private parts confirm or deny that.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْعَلَاءِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ قَالَ ‏ ‏كَانَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَسِيرُ فِي طَرِيقِ ‏ ‏مَكَّةَ ‏ ‏فَأَتَى عَلَى ‏ ‏جُمْدَانَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ هَذَا ‏ ‏جُمْدَانُ ‏ ‏سِيرُوا سَبَقَ ‏ ‏الْمُفَرِّدُونَ ‏ ‏قَالُوا وَمَا الْمُفَرِّدُونَ قَالَ الذَّاكِرُونَ اللَّهَ كَثِيرًا ثُمَّ قَالَ اللَّهُمَّ اغْفَرْ لِلْمُحَلِّقِينَ قَالُوا وَالْمُقَصِّرِينَ قَالَ ‏ ‏اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِلْمُحَلِّقِينَ قَالُوا وَالْمُقَصِّرِينَ قَالَ وَالْمُقَصِّرِينَ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us, he said: 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Ibrahim narrated to us, from al-'Ala, from his father, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), who said: The Prophet ﷺ was traveling on the road to Makkah and came upon Jumdan. He said: 'This is Jumdan. Move on! The mufarridun have surpassed.' They asked: 'Who are the mufarridun?' He said: 'Those who remember Allah abundantly.' Then he said: 'O Allah, forgive those who shave their heads.' They said: 'And those who trim?' He said: 'O Allah, forgive those who shave their heads.' They said: 'And those who trim?' He said: 'And those who trim.'

ar: ‏ ‏وَبِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لَتُؤَدُّنَّ الْحُقُوقَ إِلَى أَهْلِهَا حَتَّى ‏ ‏يُقَادَ ‏ ‏لِلشَّاةِ ‏ ‏الْجَلْحَاءِ ‏ ‏مِنْ الشَّاةِ ‏ ‏الْقَرْنَاءِ ‏
en: With the same chain of narration, he said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Rights will certainly be rendered to their owners on the Day of Resurrection, until even the hornless sheep will be granted retaliation against the horned sheep.'

ar: ‏ ‏وَبِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ ‏ ‏عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏لَا ‏ ‏يَسُومُ ‏ ‏الرَّجُلُ عَلَى ‏ ‏سَوْمِ ‏ ‏أَخِيهِ الْمُسْلِمِ وَلَا يَخْطُبُ عَلَى خِطْبَتِهِ ‏
en: With the same chain of narration, from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, who said: 'A man should not make an offer over his Muslim brother's offer, nor should he propose marriage over his brother's proposal.'

ar: ‏ ‏وَبِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏إِنَّ هَذَا الْحَرَّ مِنْ ‏ ‏فَيْحِ ‏ ‏جَهَنَّمَ ‏ ‏فَأَبْرِدُوا ‏ ‏بِالصَّلَاةِ ‏
en: With the same chain of narration, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'This heat is from the fierce blowing of Jahannam, so delay the prayer until it cools down.'

ar: ‏ ‏وَبِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِذَا سَمِعَ الشَّيْطَانُ الْأَذَانَ ‏ ‏وَلَّى ‏ ‏وَلَهُ ضُرَاطٌ حَتَّى لَا يَسْمَعَ الصَّوْتَ ‏
en: With the same chain of narration, he said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'When the Shaytan hears the adhan, he flees passing wind so that he cannot hear the call.'

ar: ‏ ‏وَبِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فُضِّلْتُ عَلَى الْأَنْبِيَاءِ بِسِتٍّ قِيلَ مَا هُنَّ أَيْ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ قَالَ ‏ ‏أُعْطِيتُ ‏ ‏جَوَامِعَ الْكَلِمِ ‏ ‏وَنُصِرْتُ ‏ ‏بِالرُّعْبِ ‏ ‏وَأُحِلَّتْ لِي الْغَنَائِمُ وَجُعِلَتْ لِي الْأَرْضُ مَسْجِدًا وَطَهُورًا وَأُرْسِلْتُ إِلَى الْخَلْقِ كَافَّةً وَخُتِمَ ‏ ‏بِيَ النَّبِيُّونَ مَثَلِي وَمَثَلُ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ عَلَيْهِمْ الصَّلَاةُ وَالسَّلَامُ كَمَثَلِ رَجُلٍ بَنَى قَصْرًا فَأَكْمَلَ بِنَاءَهُ وَأَحْسَنَ بُنْيَانَهُ إِلَّا مَوْضِعَ ‏ ‏لَبِنَةٍ ‏ ‏فَنَظَرَ النَّاسُ إِلَى الْقَصْرِ فَقَالُوا مَا أَحْسَنَ بُنْيَانَ هَذَا الْقَصْرِ لَوْ تَمَّتْ هَذِهِ ‏ ‏اللَّبِنَةُ ‏ ‏أَلَا فَكُنْتُ أَنَا ‏ ‏اللَّبِنَةَ ‏ ‏أَلَا فَكُنْتُ أَنَا ‏ ‏اللَّبِنَةَ ‏
en: With the same chain of narration, he said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'I have been favored over the Prophets with six things.' They asked: 'What are they, O Messenger of Allah?' He said: 'I have been given jawami' al-kalim (concise, comprehensive speech), I have been aided by awe (cast into the hearts of enemies), the spoils of war have been made lawful for me, the earth has been made a place of prayer and purification for me, I have been sent to all of creation, and the line of Prophets has been sealed with me. My likeness and the likeness of the Prophets (عليهم الصلاة والسلام) is that of a man who built a palace and completed its construction and beautified it, except for the place of one brick. People looked at the palace and said: How beautiful is the construction of this palace, if only this brick were completed! Indeed, I am that brick. Indeed, I am that brick.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادٌ يَعْنِي ابْنَ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سُهَيْلِ بْنِ أَبِي صَالِحٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِنَّ مِنْبَرِي عَلَى تُرْعَةٍ مِنْ ‏ ‏تُرَعِ ‏ ‏الْجَنَّةِ وَمَا بَيْنَ مِنْبَرِي وَحُجْرَتِي رَوْضَةٌ مِنْ رِيَاضِ الْجَنَّةِ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us: Hammad, meaning Ibn Salamah, narrated to us, from Suhayl ibn Abi Salih, from his father, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'My pulpit stands upon a channel from the channels of Paradise, and what is between my pulpit and my chamber is a garden from the gardens of Paradise.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْعَلَاءُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ الْعَبْدُ مَالِي وَإِنَّ مَا لَهُ مِنْ مَالِهِ ثَلَاثٌ مَا أَكَلَ فَأَفْنَى أَوْ لَبِسَ فَأَبْلَى أَوْ أَعْطَى فَأَقْنَى مَا سِوَى ذَلِكَ ذَاهِبٌ وَتَارِكُهُ لِلنَّاسِ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us: 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Ibrahim narrated to us: al-'Ala ibn 'Abd al-Rahman narrated to us, from his father, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The servant says: My wealth, my wealth! But only three things from his wealth are truly his: what he ate and consumed, what he wore and wore out, or what he gave and stored (as reward with Allah). Whatever is beyond that is departing, and he leaves it for people.'

ar: ‏ ‏وَبِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لَا ‏ ‏تَنْذِرُوا ‏ ‏فَإِنَّ ‏ ‏النَّذْرَ ‏ ‏لَا يُقَدِّمُ مِنْ ‏ ‏الْقَدَرِ ‏ ‏شَيْئًا وَإِنَّمَا يُسْتَخْرَجُ بِهِ مِنْ الْبَخِيلِ ‏
en: With the same chain of narration, he said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Do not make vows, for vows do not bring forward anything from what has been decreed. They only extract something from the miser.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ الْقَاصُّ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْعَلَاءُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَقُّ الْمُسْلِمِ عَلَى الْمُسْلِمِ سِتٌّ قَالُوا وَمَا هُنَّ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ قَالَ إِذَا لَقِيتَهُ سَلِّمْ عَلَيْهِ وَإِذَا دَعَاكَ فَأَجِبْهُ وَإِذَا اسْتَنْصَحَكَ فَانْصَحْ لَهُ وَإِذَا عَطَسَ فَحَمِدَ اللَّهَ ‏ ‏فَشَمِّتْهُ ‏ ‏وَإِذَا مَرِضَ ‏ ‏فَعُدْهُ ‏ ‏وَإِذَا مَاتَ فَاصْحَبْهُ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us, he said: 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Ibrahim al-Qass narrated to us, he said: al-'Ala ibn 'Abd al-Rahman narrated to us, from his father, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The rights of a Muslim upon another Muslim are six.' They asked: 'What are they, O Messenger of Allah?' He said: 'When you meet him, greet him with salam. When he invites you, accept his invitation. When he seeks your advice, advise him sincerely. When he sneezes and praises Allah, say yarhamuk Allah (may Allah have mercy on you). When he falls ill, visit him. And when he dies, accompany his funeral.'

ar: ‏ ‏وَبِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لَا يَجْتَمِعُ كَافِرٌ وَقَاتِلُهُ مِنْ الْمُسْلِمِينَ فِي النَّارِ أَبَدًا ‏
en: With the same chain of narration, he said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'A disbeliever and his Muslim killer will never be gathered together in the Fire.'

ar: ‏ ‏وَبِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَتَى رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏رَجُلٌ فَقَالَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏إِنَّ لِي قَرَابَةً أَصِلُهُمْ وَيَقْطَعُونِي وَأَحْلُمُ عَنْهُمْ فَيَجْهَلُونَ عَلَيَّ وَأُحْسِنُ إِلَيْهِمْ وَيُسِيئُونَ إِلَيَّ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِنْ كَانَ كَمَا تَقُولُ لَكَأَنَّمَا ‏ ‏تُسِفُّهُمْ ‏ ‏الْمَلَّ ‏ ‏وَلَا يَزَالُ مَعَكَ مِنْ اللَّهِ ظَهِيرٌ عَلَيْهِمْ مَادُمْتَ عَلَى ذَلِكَ ‏
en: With the same chain of narration, he said: A man came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, I have relatives whom I maintain ties with, but they cut me off. I treat them with forbearance, but they treat me with ignorance. I do good to them, but they do evil to me.' The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'If it is as you say, then it is as if you are feeding them hot ashes. You will continue to have a supporter from Allah against them as long as you remain upon that.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْعَلَاءُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏لَمَّا نَزَلَ عَلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ { ‏لِلَّهِ مَا فِي السَّمَوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَإِنْ تُبْدُوا مَا فِي أَنْفُسِكُمْ أَوْ تُخْفُوهُ يُحَاسِبْكُمْ بِهِ اللَّهُ فَيَغْفِرُ لِمَنْ يَشَاءُ وَيُعَذِّبُ مَنْ يَشَاءُ وَاللَّهُ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ ‏} ‏فَاشْتَدَّ ذَلِكَ عَلَى صَحَابَةِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَأَتَوْا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ ‏ ‏جَثَوْا ‏ ‏عَلَى الرُّكَبِ فَقَالُوا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ كُلِّفْنَا مِنْ الْأَعْمَالِ مَا نُطِيقُ الصَّلَاةَ وَالصِّيَامَ وَالْجِهَادَ ‏ ‏وَالصَّدَقَةَ ‏ ‏وَقَدْ أُنْزِلَ عَلَيْكَ هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ وَلَا نُطِيقُهَا فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَتُرِيدُونَ أَنْ تَقُولُوا كَمَا قَالَ أَهْلُ الْكِتَابَيْنِ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ سَمِعْنَا وَعَصَيْنَا بَلْ ‏ ‏قُولُوا سَمِعْنَا وَأَطَعْنَا غُفْرَانَكَ رَبَّنَا وَإِلَيْكَ الْمَصِيرُ فَقَالُوا سَمِعْنَا وَأَطَعْنَا غُفْرَانَكَ رَبَّنَا وَإِلَيْكَ الْمَصِيرُ فَلَمَّا أَقَرَّ بِهَا الْقَوْمُ وَذَلَّتْ بِهَا أَلْسِنَتُهُمْ أَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ فِي إِثْرِهَا ‏ { ‏آمَنَ الرَّسُولُ بِمَا أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْهِ مِنْ رَبِّهِ وَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ كُلٌّ آمَنَ بِاللَّهِ وَمَلَائِكَتِهِ وَكُتُبِهِ وَرُسُلِهِ لَا نُفَرِّقُ بَيْنَ أَحَدٍ مِنْ رُسُلِهِ ‏} ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏قَرَأَهَا ‏ ‏سَلَّامٌ أَبُو الْمُنْذِرِ ‏ ‏يُفَرِّقُ ‏ { ‏وَقَالُوا سَمِعْنَا وَأَطَعْنَا غُفْرَانَكَ رَبَّنَا وَإِلَيْكَ الْمَصِيرُ ‏} ‏فَلَمَّا فَعَلُوا ذَلِكَ ‏ ‏نَسَخَهَا ‏ ‏اللَّهُ تَبَارَكَ وَتَعَالَى بِقَوْلِهِ ‏ { ‏لَا يُكَلِّفُ اللَّهُ نَفْسًا إِلَّا ‏ ‏وُسْعَهَا ‏ ‏لَهَا مَا كَسَبَتْ وَعَلَيْهَا مَا اكْتَسَبَتْ ‏} ‏فَصَارَ لَهُ مَا كَسَبَتْ مِنْ خَيْرٍ وَعَلَيْهِ مَا اكْتَسَبَتْ مِنْ شَرٍّ فَسَّرَ ‏ ‏الْعَلَاءُ ‏ ‏هَذَا ‏ { ‏رَبَّنَا لَا تُؤَاخِذْنَا إِنْ نَسِينَا أَوْ أَخْطَأْنَا ‏} ‏قَالَ نَعَمْ ‏ { ‏رَبَّنَا وَلَا تَحْمِلْ عَلَيْنَا ‏ ‏إِصْرًا ‏ ‏كَمَا حَمَلْتَهُ عَلَى الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِنَا ‏} ‏قَالَ نَعَمْ ‏ { ‏رَبَّنَا وَلَا تُحَمِّلْنَا مَا لَا طَاقَةَ لَنَا بِهِ ‏} ‏قَالَ نَعَمْ ‏ { ‏وَاعْفُ عَنَّا وَاغْفِرْ لَنَا وَارْحَمْنَا أَنْتَ مَوْلَانَا فَانْصُرْنَا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكَافِرِينَ ‏}
en: 'Affan narrated to us, he said: 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Ibrahim narrated to us, he said: al-'Ala ibn 'Abd al-Rahman narrated to us, from his father, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), who said: When the verse was revealed to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ: "To Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Whether you show what is within yourselves or conceal it, Allah will bring you to account for it. He forgives whom He wills and punishes whom He wills, and Allah is over all things capable" [al-Baqarah 2:284], this was very difficult for the Companions of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. They came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, then knelt on their knees and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, we have been charged with deeds we can bear: prayer, fasting, jihad, and charity. But this verse has been revealed to you and we cannot bear it.' The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Do you wish to say as the People of the two Books before you said: We hear and we disobey? Rather say: We hear and we obey. Your forgiveness, our Lord, and to You is the destination.' They said: 'We hear and we obey. Your forgiveness, our Lord, and to You is the destination.' When the people affirmed this and their tongues submitted to it, Allah (عز وجل) revealed after it: 'The Messenger has believed in what was revealed to him from his Lord, and so have the believers. All of them have believed in Allah, His angels, His books, and His messengers, saying: We make no distinction between any of His messengers' [al-Baqarah 2:285]. And they said: 'We hear and we obey. Your forgiveness, our Lord, and to You is the destination.' When they did that, Allah (تبارك وتعالى) abrogated it with His saying: 'Allah does not burden a soul beyond its capacity. It will have the reward of what good it has earned, and it will bear the consequence of what evil it has earned' [al-Baqarah 2:286]. 'Our Lord, do not take us to account if we forget or make a mistake' — He said: Yes. 'Our Lord, do not lay upon us a burden as You laid upon those before us' — He said: Yes. 'Our Lord, do not burden us with that which we have no ability to bear' — He said: Yes. 'And pardon us, and forgive us, and have mercy upon us. You are our Protector, so give us victory over the disbelieving people.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْعَلَاءُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏خَرَجَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏عَلَى ‏ ‏أُبَيِّ بْنِ كَعْبٍ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ ‏ ‏يُصَلِّي فَقَالَ يَا ‏ ‏أُبَيُّ ‏ ‏فَالْتَفَتَ فَلَمْ يُجِبْهُ ثُمَّ صَلَّى ‏ ‏أُبَيٌّ ‏ ‏فَخَفَّفَ ثُمَّ انْصَرَفَ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكَ أَيْ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ قَالَ وَعَلَيْكَ قَالَ مَا مَنَعَكَ أَيْ ‏ ‏أُبَيُّ ‏ ‏إِذْ دَعَوْتُكَ أَنْ تُجِيبَنِي قَالَ أَيْ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ كُنْتُ فِي الصَّلَاةِ قَالَ أَفَلَسْتَ تَجِدُ فِيمَا أَوْحَى اللَّهُ إِلَيَّ أَنْ ‏ { ‏اسْتَجِيبُوا لِلَّهِ وَلِلرَّسُولِ إِذَا دَعَاكُمْ لِمَا يُحْيِيكُمْ ‏} ‏قَالَ قَالَ بَلَى أَيْ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ لَا أَعُودُ قَالَ أَتُحِبُّ أَنْ أُعَلِّمَكَ سُورَةً لَمْ تَنْزِلْ فِي التَّوْرَاةِ وَلَا فِي الزَّبُورِ وَلَا فِي الْإِنْجِيلِ وَلَا فِي الْفُرْقَانِ مِثْلُهَا قَالَ قُلْتُ نَعَمْ أَيْ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِنِّي لَأَرْجُو أَنْ لَا تَخْرُجَ مِنْ هَذَا الْبَابِ حَتَّى تَعْلَمَهَا قَالَ فَأَخَذَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏بِيَدِي يُحَدِّثُنِي وَأَنَا أَتَبَطَّأُ مَخَافَةَ أَنْ يَبْلُغَ قَبْلَ أَنْ ‏ ‏يَقْضِيَ ‏ ‏الْحَدِيثَ فَلَمَّا أَنْ ‏ ‏دَنَوْنَا ‏ ‏مِنْ الْبَابِ قُلْتُ أَيْ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ مَا السُّورَةُ الَّتِي وَعَدْتَنِي قَالَ فَكَيْفَ تَقْرَأُ فِي الصَّلَاةِ قَالَ فَقَرَأْتُ عَلَيْهِ ‏ ‏أُمَّ الْقُرْآنِ ‏ ‏قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَالَّذِي نَفْسِي بِيَدِهِ مَا أَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ فِي التَّوْرَاةِ وَلَا فِي الْإِنْجِيلِ وَلَا فِي الزَّبُورِ وَلَا فِي الْفُرْقَانِ مِثْلَهَا وَإِنَّهَا ‏ ‏لَلسَّبْعُ مِنْ الْمَثَانِي ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us, he said: 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Ibrahim narrated to us, he said: al-'Ala ibn 'Abd al-Rahman narrated to us, from his father, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ came out to Ubayy ibn Ka'b (رضي الله عنه) while he was praying. He said: 'O Ubayy!' Ubayy turned but did not respond, then he shortened his prayer and turned to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and said: 'Peace be upon you, O Messenger of Allah.' He replied: 'And upon you.' Then he said: 'What prevented you, O Ubayy, from responding when I called you?' He said: 'O Messenger of Allah, I was in prayer.' He said: 'Do you not find in what Allah has revealed to me: "Respond to Allah and to the Messenger when he calls you to that which gives you life" [al-Anfal 8:24]?' He said: 'Indeed, O Messenger of Allah, I will not do it again.' He said: 'Would you like me to teach you a surah the like of which has not been revealed in the Torah, nor in the Zabur, nor in the Injil, nor in the Furqan?' I said: 'Yes, O Messenger of Allah.' The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'I hope that you will not leave through this door until you learn it.' He then took me by the hand, speaking to me while I was slowing down, fearing he would reach the door before finishing. When we drew near to the door, I said: 'O Messenger of Allah, what is the surah you promised me?' He said: 'How do you recite in the prayer?' I recited to him Umm al-Quran (al-Fatihah). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'By the One in Whose Hand is my soul, Allah has not revealed the like of it in the Torah, nor in the Injil, nor in the Zabur, nor in the Furqan. It is the Seven Oft-Repeated Verses.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ثَابِتٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي رَافِعٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّ فَتًى مِنْ ‏ ‏قُرَيْشٍ ‏ ‏أَتَى ‏ ‏أَبَا هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏يَتَبَخْتَرُ فِي حُلَّةٍ لَهُ فَقَالَ سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏إِنَّ رَجُلًا مِمَّنْ كَانَ قَبْلَكُمْ كَانَ ‏ ‏يَتَبَخْتَرُ ‏ ‏فِي حُلَّةٍ لَهُ قَدْ أَعْجَبَتْهُ ‏ ‏جُمَّتُهُ ‏ ‏وَبُرْدَاهُ ‏ ‏إِذْ ‏ ‏خُسِفَ ‏ ‏بِهِ الْأَرْضُ فَهُوَ ‏ ‏يَتَجَلْجَلُ ‏ ‏فِيهَا حَتَّى تَقُومَ السَّاعَةُ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us, he said: Hammad ibn Salamah narrated to us, he said: Thabit narrated to us, from Abu Rafi', that a young man from Quraysh came to Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه) strutting in a fine garment. He said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: 'A man from those before you was strutting in a fine garment, admiring his hair and his cloak, when the earth swallowed him up, and he continues sinking in it until the Day of Resurrection.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبَانُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏قَتَادَةُ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏النَّضْرِ بْنِ أَنَسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏بَشِيرِ بْنِ نَهِيكٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏إِذَا أَفْلَسَ الرَّجُلُ ‏ ‏فَالْغَرِيمُ ‏ ‏أَحَقُّ بِمَتَاعِهِ إِذَا وَجَدَهُ بِعَيْنِهِ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us, he said: Aban ibn Yazid narrated to us, he said: Qatadah narrated to us, from al-Nadr ibn Anas, from Bashir ibn Nahik, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'When a man goes bankrupt, the creditor has more right to his goods if he finds them intact.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هَمَّامٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ أَبِي كَثِيرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏رَأَيْتُ ‏ ‏أَبَا هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏قَرَأَ ‏ ‏إِذَا السَّمَاءُ انْشَقَّتْ ‏ ‏فَسَجَدَ قُلْتُ لِمَ أَرَاكَ سَجَدْتَ فِيهَا قَالَ لَوْ لَمْ أَرَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَسْجُدُ فِيهَا مَا سَجَدْتُ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us, he said: Hammam narrated to us, he said: Yahya ibn Abi Kathir narrated to us, from Abu Salamah, who said: I saw Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه) recite Surah al-Inshiqaq and prostrate. I said: 'Why do I see you prostrating in it?' He said: 'Had I not seen the Messenger of Allah ﷺ prostrate in it, I would not have prostrated.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْعَلَاءُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏الْيَمِينُ ‏ ‏الْكَاذِبَةُ ‏ ‏مَنْفَقَةٌ ‏ ‏لِلسِّلْعَةِ ‏ ‏مَمْحَقَةٌ ‏ ‏لِلْكَسْبِ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us, he said: 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Ibrahim narrated to us, he said: al-'Ala ibn 'Abd al-Rahman narrated to us, from his father, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, who said: 'A false oath helps sell the merchandise but wipes out the earnings.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الْوَاحِدِ يَعْنِي ابْنَ زِيَادٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَاصِمُ بْنُ كُلَيْبٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏أَبَا هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏وَكَانَ يَبْتَدِئُ حَدِيثَهُ بِأَنْ يَقُولَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَبُو الْقَاسِمِ ‏ ‏الصَّادِقُ الْمَصْدُوقُ ‏ ‏مَنْ كَذَبَ عَلَيَّ مُتَعَمِّدًا ‏ ‏فَلْيَتَبَوَّأْ ‏ ‏مَقْعَدَهُ مِنْ النَّارِ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us, he said: 'Abd al-Wahid, meaning Ibn Ziyad, narrated to us, he said: 'Asim ibn Kulayb narrated to us, he said: My father narrated to me, he said: I heard Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه) saying — and he used to begin his narration by saying: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said — Abu al-Qasim, the truthful, the believed one: 'Whoever deliberately lies about me, let him take his seat in the Fire.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الْوَاحِدِ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُلَيْمَانُ الْأَعْمَشُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو صَالِحٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏أَبَا هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ ‏ ‏أَنَا عِنْدَ ظَنِّ عَبْدِي بِي وَأَنَا مَعَهُ حِينَ يَذْكُرُنِي إِنْ ذَكَرَنِي فِي نَفْسِهِ ذَكَرْتُهُ فِي نَفْسِي وَإِنْ ذَكَرَنِي فِي مَلَإٍ ذَكَرْتُهُ فِي مَلَإٍ خَيْرٍ مِنْهُ وَمَنْ تَقَرَّبَ إِلَيَّ شِبْرًا تَقَرَّبْتُ إِلَيْهِ ذِرَاعًا وَمَنْ تَقَرَّبَ إِلَيَّ ذِرَاعًا تَقَرَّبْتُ إِلَيْهِ ‏ ‏بَاعًا ‏ ‏وَمَنْ جَاءَنِي يَمْشِي جِئْتُهُ مُهَرْوِلًا ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us, he said: 'Abd al-Wahid narrated to us, he said: Sulayman al-A'mash narrated to us, he said: Abu Salih narrated to us, he said: I heard Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه) saying: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: Allah (عز وجل) said: 'I am as My servant thinks of Me, and I am with him when he remembers Me. If he remembers Me in himself, I remember him in Myself. If he remembers Me in a gathering, I remember him in a gathering better than it. Whoever draws near to Me a handspan, I draw near to him an arm's length. Whoever draws near to Me an arm's length, I draw near to him a fathom's length. And whoever comes to Me walking, I come to him running.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو عَوَانَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُهَيْلٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ إِذَا أَحَبَّ عَبْدًا دَعَا ‏ ‏جِبْرِيلَ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ ‏ ‏يَا ‏ ‏جِبْرِيلُ ‏ ‏إِنِّي أُحِبُّ فُلَانًا فَأَحِبَّهُ قَالَ فَيُحِبُّهُ ‏ ‏جِبْرِيلُ ‏ ‏عَلَيْهِ السَّلَام ‏ ‏قَالَ ثُمَّ يُنَادِي فِي أَهْلِ السَّمَاءِ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُحِبُّ فُلَانًا قَالَ فَيُحِبُّهُ أَهْلُ السَّمَاءِ ثُمَّ يُوضَعُ لَهُ الْقَبُولُ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَإِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ إِذَا أَبْغَضَ عَبْدًا دَعَا ‏ ‏جِبْرِيلَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ يَا ‏ ‏جِبْرِيلُ ‏ ‏إِنِّي أُبْغِضُ فُلَانًا فَأَبْغِضْهُ قَالَ فَيُبْغِضُهُ ‏ ‏جِبْرِيلُ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ يُنَادِي فِي أَهْلِ السَّمَاءِ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُبْغِضُ فُلَانًا فَأَبْغِضُوهُ قَالَ فَيُبْغِضُهُ أَهْلُ السَّمَاءِ ثُمَّ تُوضَعُ لَهُ الْبَغْضَاءُ فِي الْأَرْضِ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us, he said: Abu 'Awanah narrated to us, he said: Suhayl narrated to us, from his father, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: 'When Allah (عز وجل) loves a servant, He calls Jibril (عليه السلام) and says: O Jibril, I love so-and-so, so love him. So Jibril loves him. Then he calls out among the inhabitants of the heavens: Allah loves so-and-so, so love him. So the inhabitants of the heavens love him, and then acceptance is placed for him on earth. And when Allah (عز وجل) hates a servant, He calls Jibril and says: O Jibril, I hate so-and-so, so hate him. So Jibril hates him. Then he calls out among the inhabitants of the heavens: Allah hates so-and-so, so hate him. So the inhabitants of the heavens hate him, and then hatred is placed for him on earth.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وُهَيْبٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏خَالِدٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏مَا احْتَذَى النِّعَالَ وَلَا انْتَعَلَ وَلَا رَكِبَ ‏ ‏الْمَطَايَا ‏ ‏وَلَا لَبِسَ ‏ ‏الْكُورَ ‏ ‏مِنْ رَجُلٍ بَعْدَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَفْضَلُ مِنْ ‏ ‏جَعْفَرِ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ ‏ ‏يَعْنِي فِي ‏ ‏الْجُودِ ‏ ‏وَالْكَرَمِ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us, he said: Wuhayb narrated to us, he said: Khalid narrated to us, from 'Ikrimah, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), who said: 'No one who wore sandals, or rode mounts, or wore garments among men after the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was more generous than Ja'far ibn Abi Talib (رضي الله عنه) — meaning in generosity and nobility.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدٌ يَعْنِي ابْنَ سِيرِينَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏وَعَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عُمَرَ ‏ ‏أَمَّا أَحَدُهُمَا فَأَلْجَأَهُ إِلَى النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَأَمَّا الْآخَرُ فَأَلْجَأَهُ إِلَى ‏ ‏عُمَرَ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَحَدُهُمَا ‏ ‏نَهَى عَنْ ‏ ‏الزِّقَاقِ ‏ ‏وَالْمُزَفَّتِ ‏ ‏وَعَنْ ‏ ‏الدُّبَّاءِ ‏ ‏وَالْحَنْتَمِ ‏ ‏وَقَالَ الْآخَرُ نَهَى عَنْ ‏ ‏الزِّقَاقِ ‏ ‏وَالْمُزَفَّتِ ‏ ‏وَعَنْ ‏ ‏الدُّبَّاءِ ‏ ‏وَالْجَرِّ ‏ ‏أَوْ الْفَخَّارِ شَكَّ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدٌ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us, he said: Yazid ibn Ibrahim narrated to us: Muhammad, meaning Ibn Sirin, narrated to us, he said: Abu Hurayrah and 'Abdullah ibn 'Umar (رضي الله عنهما) narrated to me. One of them attributed it to the Prophet ﷺ and the other to 'Umar (رضي الله عنه). One of them said: He forbade from using skins (for nabidh), and tarred vessels, and gourds, and green jars. The other said: He forbade from skins, tarred vessels, gourds, and earthenware — Muhammad was uncertain.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُهَيْلُ بْنُ أَبِي صَالِحٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏إِذَا وَجَدَ أَحَدُكُمْ فِي صَلَاتِهِ حَرَكَةً فِي دُبُرِهِ ‏ ‏فَأَشْكَلَ ‏ ‏عَلَيْهِ ‏ ‏أَحْدَثَ ‏ ‏أَوْ لَمْ ‏ ‏يُحْدِثْ ‏ ‏فَلَا يَنْصَرِفْ حَتَّى يَسْمَعَ صَوْتًا أَوْ يَجِدَ رِيحًا ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us: Hammad ibn Salamah narrated to us, he said: Suhayl ibn Abi Salih narrated to us, from his father, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'If one of you feels movement in his posterior during prayer and is uncertain whether he has broken his wudu or not, he should not leave until he hears a sound or detects a smell.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ زَيْدٍ ‏ ‏وَصَالِحٌ الْمُعَلِّمُ ‏ ‏وَحُمَيْدٌ ‏ ‏وَيُونُسُ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْحَسَنِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏الصَّلَوَاتُ الْخَمْسُ وَالْجُمُعَةُ إِلَى الْجُمُعَةِ كَفَّارَاتٌ لِمَا بَيْنَهُنَّ مَا اجْتُنِبَتْ الْكَبَائِرُ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us, he said: Hammad ibn Salamah narrated to us, he said: 'Ali ibn Zayd, Salih al-Mu'allim, Humayd, and Yunus informed us, from al-Hasan, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The five daily prayers and Jumu'ah to Jumu'ah are expiations for what is between them, as long as major sins are avoided.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَطَاءِ بْنِ أَبِي مَيْمُونَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي رَافِعٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏كَانَ يَقُولُ ‏ ‏اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ ‏ ‏فِتْنَةِ ‏ ‏الْمَحْيَا وَالْمَمَاتِ وَمِنْ شَرِّ ‏ ‏الْمَسِيحِ الدَّجَّالِ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us: Hammad ibn Salamah narrated to us, from 'Ata ibn Abi Maymunah, from Abu Rafi', from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to say: 'O Allah, I seek refuge in You from the trials of life and death, and from the evil of al-Masih al-Dajjal.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وُهَيْبٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَيُّوبُ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْحَسَنِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏الْمُخْتَلِعَاتُ ‏ ‏وَالْمُنْتَزِعَاتُ ‏ ‏هُنَّ الْمُنَافِقَاتُ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us: Wuhayb narrated to us: Ayyub narrated to us, from al-Hasan, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: 'The women who seek khul' (divorce initiated by the wife) and the women who forcibly separate from their husbands are the hypocrites.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَطَاءِ بْنِ السَّائِبِ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْأَغَرِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِيمَا يَحْكِي عَنْ رَبِّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ قَالَ ‏ ‏الْكِبْرِيَاءُ رِدَائِي وَالْعَظَمَةُ ‏ ‏إِزَارِي ‏ ‏مَنْ نَازَعَنِي وَاحِدًا مِنْهُمَا قَذَفْتُهُ فِي النَّارِ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us: Hammad ibn Salamah narrated to us, from 'Ata ibn al-Sa'ib, from al-Agharr, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), from the Prophet ﷺ, relating from his Lord (عز وجل), who said: 'Pride is My cloak and greatness is My lower garment. Whoever contends with Me over either of them, I will cast him into the Fire.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سُهَيْلِ بْنِ أَبِي صَالِحٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏كُنْتُ أَمْشِي مَعَ أَبِي فَاطَّلَعَ ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏فِي دَارِ قَوْمٍ فَرَأَى امْرَأَةً فَقَالَ أَمَا إِنَّهُمْ لَوْ فَقَئُوا عَيْنِي لَهُدِرَتْ ثُمَّ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ سَمِعَ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏مَنْ اطَّلَعَ فِي دَارِ قَوْمٍ بِغَيْرِ إِذْنِهِمْ فَفَقَئُوا عَيْنَهُ هُدِرَتْ ‏ ‏وَقَالَ ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏مَرَّةً عَيْنٌ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us: Hammad ibn Salamah narrated to us, from Suhayl ibn Abi Salih, who said: I was walking with my father, and he looked into a people's house and saw a woman. He said: 'If they had gouged out my eye, there would be no penalty for it.' Then he said: Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه) narrated to me that he heard the Prophet ﷺ say: 'Whoever looks into a people's house without their permission and they gouge out his eye, there is no penalty for it.' 'Affan once said: 'no penalty for his eye.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏سُهَيْلُ بْنُ أَبِي صَالِحٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ دِينَارٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي صَالِحٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏الْإِيمَانُ بِضْعٌ وَسَبْعُونَ بَابًا أَفْضَلُهَا لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ ‏ ‏وَأَدْنَاهَا ‏ ‏إِمَاطَةُ ‏ ‏الْعَظْمِ عَنْ الطَّرِيقِ وَالْحَيَاءُ شُعْبَةٌ مِنْ الْإِيمَانِ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us, he said: Hammad ibn Salamah narrated to us, he said: Suhayl ibn Abi Salih informed us, from 'Abdullah ibn Dinar, from Abu Salih, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Iman (faith) has seventy-some branches. The highest of them is La ilaha illa Allah (there is no god but Allah), and the lowest is removing a bone from the road. And modesty is a branch of faith.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُعَاذُ بْنُ هِشَامٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏قَتَادَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏زُرَارَةَ بْنِ أَوْفَى ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ نَبِيَّ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏لَا تَصْحَبُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ رُفْقَةً فِيهَا جَرَسٌ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us: Mu'adh ibn Hisham narrated to us: my father narrated to us, from Qatadah, from Zurarah ibn Awfa, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), that the Prophet of Allah ﷺ said: 'The angels do not accompany a group in which there is a bell.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الْوَارِثِ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ زَيْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيَّبِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ إِنَّ رَبَّكُمْ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ يَقُولُ ‏ ‏يَا ابْنَ ‏ ‏آدَمَ ‏ ‏بِكُلِّ حَسَنَةٍ عَشْرُ حَسَنَاتٍ إِلَى سَبْعِ مِائَةِ ضِعْفٍ إِلَى أَضْعَافٍ كَثِيرَةٍ وَالصَّوْمُ لِي وَأَنَا أَجْزِي بِهِ وَالصَّوْمُ ‏ ‏جُنَّةٌ ‏ ‏مِنْ النَّارِ ‏ ‏وَلَخُلُوفُ ‏ ‏فَمِ الصَّائِمِ أَطْيَبُ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ مِنْ رِيحِ الْمِسْكِ فَإِنْ جَهِلَ عَلَى أَحَدِكُمْ جَاهِلٌ وَهُوَ صَائِمٌ فَلْيَقُلْ إِنِّي صَائِمٌ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us: 'Abd al-Warith narrated to us: 'Ali ibn Zayd informed us, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: 'Your Lord (عز وجل) says: O son of Adam, for every good deed there are ten good deeds up to seven hundred fold, up to many more folds. And fasting is for Me and I reward for it. Fasting is a shield from the Fire. The breath of the fasting person is more pleasant to Allah (عز وجل) than the fragrance of musk. If an ignorant person acts with ignorance toward one of you while he is fasting, let him say: I am fasting.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏أَبَا هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَبُو الْقَاسِمِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لَوْ سَلَكَتْ ‏ ‏الْأَنْصَارُ ‏ ‏وَادِيًا ‏ ‏أَوْ ‏ ‏شِعْبًا ‏ ‏لَسَلَكْتُ ‏ ‏شِعْبَ ‏ ‏الْأَنْصَارِ ‏ ‏أَوْ وَادِيَ ‏ ‏الْأَنْصَارِ ‏ ‏وَلَوْلَا الْهِجْرَةُ لَكُنْتُ امْرَأً مِنْ ‏ ‏الْأَنْصَارِ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ ‏ ‏أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏فَمَا ظَلَمَ بِأَبِي وَأُمِّي ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لَآوَوْهُ وَنَصَرُوهُ ‏ ‏قَالَ وَأَحْسَبُهُ قَالَ وَوَاسَوْهُ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us: Shu'bah narrated to us, from Muhammad ibn Ziyad, who said: I heard Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه) saying: Abu al-Qasim ﷺ said: 'If the Ansar were to take a valley or a mountain pass, I would take the mountain pass of the Ansar — or the valley of the Ansar. Were it not for the Hijrah, I would have been a man of the Ansar.' Abu Hurayrah said: 'He did them no injustice — by my father and mother ﷺ — they gave him shelter and supported him.' He said: 'And I think he also said: and shared with him.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏عَلْقَمَةُ بْنُ مَرْثَدٍ ‏ ‏أَنْبَأَنِي قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏أَبَا الرَّبِيعِ ‏ ‏يُحَدِّثُ أَنَّهُ سَمِعَ ‏ ‏أَبَا هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَرْبَعٌ فِي أُمَّتِي لَنْ يَدَعُوهَا ‏ ‏التَّطَاعُنُ ‏ ‏فِي الْأَنْسَابِ ‏ ‏وَالنِّيَاحَةُ ‏ ‏وَمُطِرْنَا ‏ ‏بِنَوْءِ ‏ ‏كَذَا وَكَذَا اشْتَرَيْتَ بَعِيرًا أَجْرَبَ ‏ ‏أَوْ فَجَرِبَ ‏ ‏فَجَعَلْتَهُ فِي مِائَةِ بَعِيرٍ فَجَرِبَتْ مَنْ أَعْدَى الْأَوَّلَ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us: Shu'bah narrated to us, who said: 'Alqamah ibn Marthad informed me, who said: I heard Abu al-Rabi' narrating that he heard Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: 'Four things are from the Jahiliyyah that my ummah will not abandon: disparaging lineages, wailing over the dead, attributing rain to the stars saying "we were given rain by such-and-such star," and (saying) you bought a mangy camel and put it among a hundred camels and they all got mange — so who infected the first one?'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَاسِمُ بْنُ مِهْرَانَ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنِيهِ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏أَبَا رَافِعٍ ‏ ‏يُحَدِّثُ عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏رَأَى ‏ ‏نُخَامَةً ‏ ‏فِي الْقِبْلَةِ ‏ ‏قَالَ كَانَ يَقُولُ مَرَّةً فَحَتَّهَا ‏ ‏قَالَ ثُمَّ قَالَ قُمْتُ ‏ ‏فَحَتَّيْتُهَا ثُمَّ قَالَ ‏ ‏أَيُحِبُّ أَحَدُكُمْ إِذَا كَانَ فِي صَلَاتِهِ أَنْ ‏ ‏يُتَنَخَّعَ ‏ ‏فِي وَجْهِهِ ‏ ‏أَوْ يُبْزَقَ فِي وَجْهِهِ ‏ ‏إِذَا كَانَ أَحَدُكُمْ فِي صَلَاتِهِ فَلَا يَبْزُقَنَّ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ وَلَا عَنْ يَمِينِهِ وَلَكِنْ عَنْ يَسَارِهِ تَحْتَ قَدَمِهِ فَإِنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ قَالَ بِثَوْبِهِ هَكَذَا ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us, he said: Shu'bah narrated to us, who said: Qasim ibn Mihran informed me, who said: I heard Abu Rafi' narrating from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), that the Prophet ﷺ saw phlegm on the wall facing the qiblah. He said — he used to say at times — he scraped it off. Then he said: I got up and scraped it off. Then he said: 'Would any of you like it if, while in prayer, someone spat in his face or blew mucus in his face? When any of you is in prayer, he should not spit in front of him or to his right, but to his left under his foot. If he cannot find a place, then into his garment like this.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏يُوشِكُ أَنْ ‏ ‏يَحْسِرَ ‏ ‏الْفُرَاتُ عَنْ جَبَلٍ مِنْ ذَهَبٍ فَيَقْتَتِلَ عَلَيْهِ النَّاسُ حَتَّى يُقْتَلَ مِنْ كُلِّ عَشَرَةٍ تِسْعَةٌ وَيَبْقَى وَاحِدٌ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us: Hammad ibn Salamah narrated to us: Muhammad ibn 'Amr narrated to us, from Abu Salamah, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The Euphrates is about to uncover a mountain of gold, and people will fight over it until nine out of every ten are killed, and one remains.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الْوَاحِدِ بْنُ زِيَادٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُلَيْمَانُ الْأَعْمَشُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي صَالِحٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِذَا صَلَّى أَحَدُكُمْ الرَّكْعَتَيْنِ قَبْلَ صَلَاةِ الصُّبْحِ فَلْيَضْطَجِعْ عَلَى جَنْبِهِ الْأَيْمَنِ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us: 'Abd al-Wahid ibn Ziyad narrated to us: Sulayman al-A'mash narrated to us, from Abu Salih, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'When one of you prays the two rak'ahs before the Fajr prayer, let him lie down on his right side.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وُهَيْبٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هِشَامٌ يَعْنِي ابْنَ عُرْوَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏رَجُلٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏إِذَا أَكَلَ أَحَدُكُمْ فَلْيَلْعَقْ أَصَابِعَهُ فَإِنَّهُ لَا يَدْرِي فِي أَيِّ ذَلِكَ الْبَرَكَةُ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us: Wuhayb narrated to us: Hisham, meaning Ibn 'Urwah, narrated to us, from a man, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'When one of you eats, let him lick his fingers, for he does not know in which part of the food the blessing lies.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏أَبَا هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏الْعَجْمَاءُ ‏ ‏جَرْحُهَا ‏ ‏جُبَارٌ ‏ ‏وَالْمَعْدِنُ ‏ ‏جُبَارٌ ‏ ‏وَالْبِئْرُ ‏ ‏جُبَارٌ ‏ ‏وَفِي ‏ ‏الرِّكَازِ ‏ ‏الْخُمْسُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مِثْلَ هَذَا غَيْرَ أَنَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏الرَّكَائِزُ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us: Shu'bah narrated to us, from Muhammad ibn Ziyad, who said: I heard Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: 'The injury caused by an animal is jubar (no liability), and the mine is jubar, and the well is jubar, and in rikaz (buried treasure) there is one-fifth.' 'Affan narrated to us: Shu'bah narrated to us: Muhammad ibn 'Amr narrated to us, from Abu Salamah, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), from the Prophet ﷺ, a similar narration except that he said 'al-raka'iz' (plural of rikaz).

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْقَاسِمُ بْنُ الْفَضْلِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُعَاوِيَةَ الْمَهْرِيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ قَالَ ‏ ‏أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏يَا مَهْرِيُّ ‏ ‏نَهَى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ثَمَنِ الْكَلْبِ وَكَسْبِ ‏ ‏الْمُومِسَةِ ‏ ‏وَكَسْبِ الْحَجَّامِ ‏ ‏وَكَسْبِ ‏ ‏عَسِيبِ ‏ ‏الْفَحْلِ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us: al-Qasim ibn al-Fadl narrated to us: my father narrated to me, from Mu'awiyah al-Mahri, who said: Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه) said: 'O Mahri! The Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade the price of a dog, the earnings of a prostitute, the earnings of a cupper, and the stud fee of a stallion.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ زَيْدٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْعَبَّاسُ الْجُرَيْرِيُّ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏أَبَا عُثْمَانَ النَّهْدِيَّ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏تَضَيَّفْتُ ‏ ‏أَبَا هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏سَبْعًا قَالَ وَسَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ ‏ ‏قَسَمَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏بَيْنَ أَصْحَابِهِ تَمْرًا فَأَصَابَنِي سَبْعُ تَمَرَاتٍ إِحْدَاهُنَّ ‏ ‏حَشَفَةٌ ‏ ‏فَلَمْ يَكُنْ شَيْءٌ ‏ ‏أَعْجَبَ ‏ ‏إِلَيَّ مِنْهَا شَدَّتْ ‏ ‏مَضَاغِي ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us: Hammad ibn Zayd narrated to us: al-'Abbas al-Jurayri narrated to us, who said: I heard Abu 'Uthman al-Nahdi saying: I was a guest of Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه) for seven days. He said: I heard him say: The Prophet ﷺ distributed dates among his companions, and I received seven dates, one of which was a dried-up date. Nothing was more beloved to me than it, for it strengthened my chewing.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادٌ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏ثَابِتٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي رَافِعٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏لَا يَزَالُ الْعَبْدُ فِي صَلَاةٍ مَا كَانَ فِي مُصَلَّاهُ يَنْتَظِرُ الصَّلَاةَ تَقُولُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لَهُ اللَّهُمَّ ارْحَمْهُ حَتَّى يَنْصَرِفَ أَوْ يُحْدِثَ قُلْتُ وَمَا يُحْدِثُ قَالَ ‏ ‏يَفْسُو أَوْ يَضْرِطُ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us: Hammad narrated to us: Thabit informed us, from Abu Rafi', from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'A servant continues to be in a state of prayer as long as he is in his place of prayer waiting for the prayer. The angels say: O Allah, forgive him. O Allah, have mercy on him, until he leaves or breaks his wudu.' I asked: 'What does breaking wudu mean?' He said: 'Passing wind silently or audibly.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ زَيْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيَّبِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏يَدْخُلُ أَهْلُ الْجَنَّةِ ‏ ‏مُرْدًا ‏ ‏بِيضًا ‏ ‏جِعَادًا ‏ ‏مُكَحَّلِينَ أَبْنَاءَ ثَلَاثٍ وَثَلَاثِينَ عَلَى خَلْقِ ‏ ‏آدَمَ ‏ ‏سَبْعِينَ ذِرَاعًا فِي سَبْعَةِ أَذْرُعٍ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us: Hammad ibn Salamah narrated to us, he said: 'Ali ibn Zayd informed us, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: 'The people of Paradise will enter it beardless, white-skinned, with curly hair, with kohl-lined eyes, at the age of thirty-three, in the form of Adam (عليه السلام), seventy cubits tall and seven cubits wide.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ زِيَادٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏أَبَا هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏أَبَا الْقَاسِمِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏صُومُوا الْهِلَالَ لِرُؤْيَتِهِ وَأَفْطِرُوا لِرُؤْيَتِهِ فَإِنْ ‏ ‏غُمَّ ‏ ‏عَلَيْكُمْ فَعُدُّوا ثَلَاثِينَ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us: Hammad ibn Salamah narrated to us: Muhammad ibn Ziyad informed us, who said: I heard Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه) saying: I heard Abu al-Qasim ﷺ say: 'Begin fasting upon sighting the crescent moon and break your fast upon sighting it. If it is obscured from you, then complete the count of thirty days.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَدِيِّ بْنِ ثَابِتٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي حَازِمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏الْمُؤْمِنُ يَأْكُلُ فِي مِعًى وَاحِدٍ وَالْكَافِرُ يَأْكُلُ فِي سَبْعَةِ أَمْعَاءٍ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us: Shu'bah narrated to us, from 'Adi ibn Thabit, from Abu Hazim, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: 'The believer eats with one intestine, and the disbeliever eats with seven intestines.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الْوَاحِدِ بْنُ زِيَادٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عُمَارَةُ بْنُ الْقَعْقَاعِ بْنِ شُبْرُمَةَ الضَّبِّيُّ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو زُرْعَةَ بْنُ عَمْرِو بْنِ جَرِيرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏جَاءَ رَجُلٌ إِلَى النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ أَيُّ ‏ ‏الصَّدَقَةِ ‏ ‏أَعْظَمُ قَالَ ‏ ‏أَنْ تَتَصَدَّقَ وَأَنْتَ صَحِيحٌ ‏ ‏شَحِيحٌ ‏ ‏تَخْشَى الْفَقْرَ وَتَأْمُلُ الْبَقَاءَ وَلَا تَمَهَّلْ حَتَّى إِذَا بَلَغَتْ الْحُلْقُومَ قُلْتَ لِفُلَانٍ كَذَا وَلِفُلَانٍ كَذَا وَقَدْ كَانَ لِفُلَانٍ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us: 'Abd al-Wahid ibn Ziyad narrated to us, he said: 'Umarah ibn al-Qa'qa' ibn Shubramah al-Dabbi narrated to us, he said: Abu Zur'ah ibn 'Amr ibn Jarir informed us, he said: Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه) narrated to us, he said: A man came to the Prophet ﷺ and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, which charity is the greatest?' He said: 'That you give charity while you are healthy, covetous, fearing poverty, and hoping for a long life. Do not delay until the soul reaches the throat and you say: Give to so-and-so such-and-such, and give to so-and-so such-and-such — when it already belongs to so-and-so.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏ثَابِتٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي رَافِعٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كَانَتْ شَجَرَةٌ تُؤْذِي أَهْلَ الطَّرِيقِ فَقَطَعَهَا رَجُلٌ فَنَحَّاهَا فَدَخَلَ الْجَنَّةَ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us, he said: Hammad narrated to us, he said: Thabit informed us, from Abu Rafi', from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'There was a tree that used to harm the people on the road. A man cut it down and moved it aside, and he entered Paradise.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏إِنَّ الْكَرِيمَ ابْنَ الْكَرِيمِ ابْنِ الْكَرِيمِ ابْنِ الْكَرِيمِ ‏ ‏يُوسُفُ بْنُ يَعْقُوبَ بْنِ إِسْحَقَ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ أَجْمَعِينَ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us: Hammad ibn Salamah narrated to us, from Muhammad ibn 'Amr, from Abu Salamah, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The noblest, son of the noblest, son of the noblest, son of the noblest: Yusuf ibn Ya'qub ibn Ishaq ibn Ibrahim (صلى الله عليهم أجمعين).'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏دَاوُدُ بْنُ فَرَاهِيجَ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏أَبَا هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏مَا كَانَ لَنَا عَلَى عَهْدِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏طَعَامٌ إِلَّا الْأَسْوَدَانِ التَّمْرُ وَالْمَاءُ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us: Shu'bah narrated to us: Dawud ibn Farahij narrated to us, who said: I heard Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه) say: 'We had no food during the time of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ except the two black things: dates and water.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ الْمُهَاجِرِ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنِي قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏أَبَا الشَّعْثَاءِ الْمُحَارِبِيَّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كُنَّا مَعَ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏فِي مَسْجِدٍ ‏ ‏فَخَرَجَ رَجُلٌ وَقَدْ أَذَّنَ الْمُؤَذِّنُ قَالَ فَقَالَ أَمَّا هَذَا فَقَدْ عَصَى ‏ ‏أَبَا الْقَاسِمِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us: Shu'bah narrated to us, who said: Ibrahim ibn al-Muhajir informed me, who said: I heard Abu al-Sha'tha al-Muharibi say: We were with Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه) in a mosque when a man left after the mu'adhdhin had called the adhan. He said: 'As for this one, he has disobeyed Abu al-Qasim ﷺ.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَاصِمِ بْنِ بَهْدَلَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي صَالِحٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَخَّرَ الْعِشَاءَ الْآخِرَةَ ذَاتَ لَيْلَةٍ حَتَّى كَادَ يَذْهَبُ ثُلُثُ اللَّيْلِ ‏ ‏أَوْ قُرَابُهُ ‏ ‏قَالَ ثُمَّ جَاءَ وَفِي النَّاسِ ‏ ‏رِقَّةٌ ‏ ‏وَهُمْ ‏ ‏عِزُونَ ‏ ‏فَغَضِبَ غَضَبًا شَدِيدًا ثُمَّ قَالَ ‏ ‏لَوْ أَنَّ رَجُلًا ‏ ‏بَدَا ‏ ‏النَّاسَ إِلَى ‏ ‏عَرْقٍ ‏ ‏أَوْ ‏ ‏مِرْمَاتَيْنِ ‏ ‏لَأَجَابُوا لَهُ وَهُمْ يَتَخَلَّفُونَ عَنْ هَذِهِ الصَّلَاةِ لَقَدْ هَمَمْتُ أَنْ آمُرَ رَجُلًا فَيَتَخَلَّفَ عَلَى أَهْلِ هَذِهِ الدُّورِ الَّذِينَ يَتَخَلَّفُونَ عَنْ هَذِهِ الصَّلَاةِ فَأُحَرِّقَهَا عَلَيْهِمْ بِالنِّيرَانِ ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us: Hammad ibn Salamah narrated to us, from 'Asim ibn Bahdala, from Abu Salih, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ delayed the 'Isha prayer one night until nearly a third of the night had passed, or close to it. Then he came out and the people were few, gathered in groups. He became very angry, then said: 'If a man were to invite people to a bone or a pair of sheep trotters, they would respond to him, yet they stay away from this prayer. I have considered ordering a man to lead the prayer while I go to the houses of those who stay behind from this prayer and burn them down upon them with fire.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو المُهَزِّمِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَمَرَ ‏ ‏فَاطِمَةَ ‏ ‏أَوْ ‏ ‏أُمَّ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏أَنْ تَجُرَّ ذَيْلَهَا ذِرَاعًا ‏
en: 'Affan narrated to us: Hammad ibn Salamah narrated to us, he said: Abu al-Muhazzim informed us, from Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه), that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ ordered Fatimah — or Umm Salamah — to let her train (the trailing end of her garment) extend a cubit.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏وَبَهْزٌ ‏ ‏قَالَا حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو عَوَانَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَعْلَى بْنِ عَطَاءٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي عَلْقَمَةَ الْأَنْصَارِيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏مِنْ ‏ ‏فِيهِ إِلَى ‏ ‏فِيَّ قَالَ ‏ ‏سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏مَنْ أَطَاعَنِي فَقَدْ أَطَاعَ اللَّهَ وَمَنْ عَصَانِي فَقَدْ عَصَى اللَّهَ وَمَنْ أَطَاعَ الْأَمِيرَ فَقَدْ أَطَاعَنِي إِنَّمَا الْأَمِيرُ ‏ ‏مِجَنٌّ ‏ ‏فَإِنْ صَلَّى جَالِسًا فَصَلُّوا جُلُوسًا أَوْ قُعُودًا فَإِذَا قَالَ سَمِعَ اللَّهُ لِمَنْ حَمِدَهُ فَقُولُوا اللَّهُمَّ رَبَّنَا لَكَ الْحَمْدُ فَإِنَّهُ إِذَا وَافَقَ قَوْلُ أَهْلِ الْأَرْضِ قَوْلَ أَهْلِ السَّمَاءِ غُفِرَ لَهُ مَا مَضَى مِنْ ذَنْبِهِ ‏
en: 'Affan and Bahz narrated to us, they said: Abu 'Awanah narrated to us, from Ya'la ibn 'Ata, from Abu 'Alqamah al-Ansari, who said: Abu Hurayrah (رضي الله عنه) narrated to me directly, mouth to mouth, he said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ saying: 'Whoever obeys me has obeyed Allah, and whoever disobeys me has disobeyed Allah. Whoever obeys the amir has obeyed me. The imam is but a shield. If he prays sitting, then pray sitting. When he says "Sami'a Allahu liman hamidah," say "Allahumma Rabbana lakal-hamd." For when the words of the people of the earth coincide with the words of the people of the heavens, his past sins are forgiven.'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏وَيَهْلِكُ ‏ ‏قَيْصَرُ ‏ ‏فَلَا يَكُونُ ‏ ‏قَيْصَرُ ‏ ‏بَعْدَهُ وَيَهْلِكُ ‏ ‏كِسْرَى ‏ ‏فَلَا يَكُونُ ‏ ‏كِسْرَى ‏ ‏بَعْدَهُ ‏
en: He said: 'Caesar will perish, and there will be no Caesar after him. Kisra (Chosroes) will perish, and there will be no Kisra after him.'

ar: ‏ ‏وَقَالَ ‏ ‏اسْتَعِيذُوا بِاللَّهِ مِنْ خَمْسٍ مِنْ عَذَابِ جَهَنَّمَ وَعَذَابِ الْقَبْرِ ‏ ‏وَفِتْنَةِ ‏ ‏الْمَحْيَا وَالْمَمَاتِ ‏ ‏وَفِتْنَةِ ‏ ‏الْمَسِيحِ الدَّجَّالِ ‏
